Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-09 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: Momentum Ranker Data Today's data reveals an extraordinary outlier in ERAS, which dominates the ranking with a score of 97.6 and rank #1, posting a 978% one-year return alongside perfect performance across all technical gates (above the 30/50/100/200-day SMAs). The ratio consistency is flawless at 5 passes, and ERAS maintains a remarkable 961% outperformance versus SPY over the past year, though this extreme momentum is paired with a thin volatility profile (vol_ratio: 0.511), suggesting low trading volume relative to price movement. In contrast, CNQ scores lower but represents a more conventional momentum profile—a 75.62% one-year return with strong ratio passes (4/5) and consistent outperformance across benchmarks, particularly showing 58.72% beats versus SPY. The correlation vectors show ERAS is moving independently from traditional sectors (negative correlations with financials, utilities, and communications), while CNQ exhibits expected sector alignment (0.615 correlation with XLE energy sector). Both tickers clear all SMA gates and demonstrate resilience versus SPY on down days, but ERAS's extreme returns and thin liquidity present a markedly different risk-reward profile than CNQ's steadier momentum.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
